articles.headline: Union Budget 2025-26: Focus on Infrastructure, Green Growth, and Fiscal Prudence
articles.explainer:
• Following its presentation in Parliament yesterday, the Union Budget 2025-26 is being widely analyzed for its strategic thrust on capital expenditure and sustainable development.
• The budget prioritizes continued investment in critical infrastructure projects, including railways, highways, and urban development, aiming to boost economic activity and job creation.
• A significant allocation has been made towards green initiatives, including renewable energy projects, electric vehicle infrastructure, and climate resilience programs, aligning with India's global commitments.
• The Finance Minister reiterated the government's commitment to fiscal consolidation, targeting a reduced fiscal deficit while ensuring adequate resources for social welfare schemes.
• Experts highlight the budget's balanced approach, seeking to stimulate growth through public investment without compromising long-term fiscal stability.

articles.trivia: India's first Union Budget was presented on February 28, 1860, by James Wilson, a Scottish economist and politician.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to Review Implementation Challenges of Digital Personal Data Protection Act
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has announced it will hear a petition seeking clarity and addressing implementation challenges related to the recently enacted Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023.
• Petitioners highlight concerns regarding the readiness of data fiduciaries, the establishment of the Data Protection Board, and the clarity of certain provisions.
• The court's intervention is crucial for ensuring effective enforcement of the act and safeguarding citizens' privacy rights in the digital age.
• Legal experts anticipate the ruling will provide much-needed guidance on the operational aspects and potential ambiguities within the landmark legislation.
• The case underscores the judiciary's role in overseeing the execution of significant legislative reforms impacting fundamental rights.

Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
Right to Privacy

The Act establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
Key principles: consent, data minimization, purpose limitation.
Right to be forgotten and right to correction/erasure are provisions.

mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the DPDP Act, 2023, in the context of India's digital economy and citizens' rights.
Analyze the potential challenges in the implementation of the DPDP Act, including technological, legal, and infrastructural hurdles.
Examine how the Supreme Court's review could shape the future of data governance and privacy jurisprudence in India.

Suggest measures to enhance public awareness and compliance with the DPDP Act.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a legal book with digital data streams in the background, symbolizing digital law and justice.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) by the Supreme Court declared the right to privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes: Focus on Defence, Space, and Indo-Pacific Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded today in New Delhi, reaffirming the strong bilateral partnership between the two nations.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in critical sectors such as defence manufacturing, space exploration, and emerging technologies.
• Both sides stressed the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and agreed to further coordinate efforts for regional stability and security.
• Agreements were reached to bolster joint exercises, technology transfer, and collaborative research and development in defence.
• The dialogue underscored the shared commitment to multilateralism and addressing global challenges, including climate change and counter-terrorism.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Ocean Monitoring Satellite, Enhancing Maritime Security and Climate Research
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched a new advanced ocean monitoring satellite from Sriharikota, marking another milestone in India's space program.
• The satellite is equipped with high-resolution sensors to provide critical data on sea surface temperature, ocean currents, and marine ecosystems, vital for climate research and disaster prediction.
• This mission will significantly enhance India's capabilities in maritime domain awareness, assisting coastal security and naval operations.
• The data collected will also be crucial for fisheries management, monsoon prediction, and understanding the impacts of climate change on oceans.
• The launch further solidifies India's position as a leader in space technology for societal applications and environmental monitoring.

articles.trivia: The first Indian satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

articles.headline: National Mission for Clean Ganga Releases Progress Report, Highlights Achievements in River Rejuvenation
articles.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) has released its annual progress report, detailing significant strides in the rejuvenation of the Ganga River and its tributaries.
• The report highlights achievements in wastewater treatment infrastructure, solid waste management along the river banks, and afforestation drives.
• Increased community participation and public awareness campaigns have been crucial in fostering a sense of ownership for the 'Namami Gange' program.
• Challenges remain in addressing industrial pollution and ensuring sustained maintenance of assets, necessitating continued vigilance and integrated efforts.
• The mission aims to extend its focus to tributary rejuvenation and wetlands conservation, adopting a holistic approach to river basin management.

articles.headline: Tragic Bus Accident in Gujarat Claims Five Lives, Injures 35 Pilgrims
articles.explainer:
• A devastating bus accident occurred near Saputara, Gujarat, when a bus carrying pilgrims veered off the road and fell into a gorge.
• The incident resulted in the tragic deaths of five individuals and left 35 others injured, some critically, prompting immediate rescue operations.
• Local authorities, along with National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) teams, were swiftly deployed to the site for rescue and relief efforts.
• The pilgrims were reportedly returning from a religious site when the accident took place, with preliminary investigations pointing towards possible driver fatigue or mechanical failure.
• The state government has announced ex-gratia payments for the victims' families and assured comprehensive medical treatment for the injured.

mainsPoints: Discuss the persistent challenges in ensuring road safety in India, particularly for public transport and pilgrimage routes.
Analyze the role of various stakeholders, including government, transport authorities, and vehicle manufacturers, in preventing such accidents.
Examine the effectiveness of current disaster response mechanisms in handling large-scale road accidents.
Suggest comprehensive measures, including policy reforms and technological interventions, to improve road safety standards in the country.
Discuss the socio-economic impact of such tragedies on affected families and communities.
articles.imageDescription: Rescue workers assisting victims at an accident site with an overturned bus in a gorge.
articles.trivia: India accounts for about 11% of the total road accident deaths in the world, despite having only 1% of the world's vehicles.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework to Boost MSME Exports
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) has launched a new policy framework aimed at significantly boosting exports from the sector.
• The policy includes provisions for easier access to credit, export promotion schemes, technological upgrades, and participation in international trade fairs.
• Special focus will be given to integrating MSMEs into global value chains and providing training for export readiness and compliance with international standards.
• This initiative is expected to enhance India's overall export competitiveness and contribute to the 'Make in India' vision by empowering smaller enterprises.
• Industry bodies have welcomed the move, emphasizing its potential to create jobs and foster economic diversification.

articles.headline: RBI Monetary Policy Committee Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest review meeting, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision comes amidst persistent concerns over retail inflation, despite signs of moderation in certain sectors of the economy.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, indicating a cautious 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Analysts suggest that the RBI is closely monitoring global economic developments and domestic demand conditions before considering any policy rate changes.
• The stability in the repo rate aims to provide certainty to financial markets and support the economic recovery trajectory.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC meets bi-monthly.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks.
Inflation target set by government is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.

MPC consists of 6 members (3 RBI, 3 government nominees).
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst current economic conditions.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Grameen Kaushal Vikas Yojana' to Empower Rural Youth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship has launched the 'Grameen Kaushal Vikas Yojana' (GKVY), a new scheme aimed at enhancing skill development among rural youth.
• The program focuses on providing vocational training in demand-driven sectors, facilitating apprenticeships, and linking trainees with employment opportunities in local industries.
• GKVY targets school dropouts and unemployed youth in rural areas, offering certified courses in various trades relevant to the rural economy and emerging sectors.
• The scheme emphasizes public-private partnerships and leveraging digital platforms for wider outreach and efficient delivery of training programs.
• This initiative is expected to address the skill gap in rural India, reduce rural-urban migration, and boost rural entrepreneurship.

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Introduces Policy to Promote Circular Economy Principles
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has introduced a new national policy aimed at promoting circular economy principles across various sectors.
• The policy encourages waste reduction, reuse, recycling, and resource efficiency, moving away from the traditional linear 'take-make-dispose' model.
• Key provisions include incentives for industries adopting circular practices, development of eco-design standards, and promotion of green procurement.
• This initiative is expected to reduce environmental degradation, conserve natural resources, and create new economic opportunities in green industries.
• Stakeholders emphasize the need for robust regulatory frameworks and technological innovation to successfully transition towards a circular economy.

articles.headline: Ministry of AYUSH Unveils New Policy for Promotion and Integration of Traditional Indian Medicine Systems
articles.explainer:

• It seeks to leverage the potential of AYUSH systems for preventive healthcare, wellness, and managing chronic diseases, complementing modern medicine.
• The initiative includes provisions for international collaboration to globalize traditional Indian medicine and establish India as a hub for AYUSH healthcare.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Accelerated Melting of Himalayan Glaciers, Urges Urgent Action
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by a consortium of environmental scientists highlights an alarming acceleration in the melting rate of Himalayan glaciers.
• The study attributes the intensified melting to rising global temperatures and localized black carbon deposition, threatening water security for millions.
• The rapid glacial retreat poses severe risks of increased flash floods, subsequent water scarcity, and long-term ecological imbalance in the region.
• Scientists call for urgent and concerted efforts to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and implement targeted conservation strategies in the Himalayan ecosystem.
• The report emphasizes the critical need for regional cooperation among Himalayan nations to address this transboundary environmental challenge.

mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and socio-economic implications of accelerated Himalayan glacial melting for India and the wider South Asian region.

articles.headline: India's First Private Sector Developed Cryogenic Rocket Engine Successfully Tested

• This achievement marks a major step towards private players contributing to India's space launch capabilities, reducing reliance on ISRO for all components.
• The engine, developed by a Bengaluru-based startup, demonstrates the growing maturity and innovation within India's space tech ecosystem.
• This success is expected to attract further investment into the private space sector, fostering competition and accelerating technological advancements.

articles.headline: New Archaeological Discoveries at Harappan Site Shed Light on Ancient Trade Routes
articles.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations at a lesser-known Harappan site in Rajasthan have unearthed new artifacts, providing fresh insights into ancient trade routes and cultural exchanges.
• The discoveries include unique pottery, seals, and tools that suggest extensive trade networks extending beyond the Indus Valley, possibly connecting with Mesopotamian civilizations.
• These findings challenge previous assumptions about the geographical limits and economic practices of the Harappan civilization.
• Archaeologists believe the site served as a crucial transit point, facilitating the exchange of goods and ideas between various ancient cultures.
• Further analysis of the artifacts is expected to refine our understanding of the socio-economic structure and inter-regional interactions of the Bronze Age in India.

articles.headline: Government Launches National Urban Air Mobility Policy to Boost Drone and Air Taxi Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Civil Aviation has unveiled India's first National Urban Air Mobility (NUAM) Policy, aiming to regulate and promote the use of drones and air taxis for urban transportation.
• The policy outlines a framework for safe operation, infrastructure development, air traffic management, and skill development for the emerging urban air mobility sector.
• This initiative is expected to revolutionize intra-city logistics, emergency services, and passenger transport, reducing congestion and travel times.
• It seeks to position India as a leader in drone technology and air taxi services, attracting investment and fostering innovation in the aerospace industry.
• Challenges include ensuring public safety, managing airspace, and developing robust charging infrastructure for electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Union Budget 2025-26, consider the following statements:

prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Union Budget is typically presented on February 1st. The ePaper date is February 2nd, which would be for analysis post-presentation. prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023:
1. It establishes the Data Protection Board of India.
2. It includes provisions for the 'right to be forgotten'.
3. The Act primarily focuses on data generated by government entities only.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The DPDP Act, 2023 establishes the Data Protection Board of India and includes provisions like the right to be forgotten. Statement 3 is incorrect; the Act covers personal data processed by both government and private entities, extending beyond just government entities.

GS1.headline: Tragic Bus Accident in Gujarat Claims Five Lives, Injures 35 Pilgrims
GS1.explainer:
• A devastating bus accident occurred near Saputara, Gujarat, when a bus carrying pilgrims veered off the road and fell into a gorge.
• The incident resulted in the tragic deaths of five individuals and left 35 others injured, some critically, prompting immediate rescue operations.
• Local authorities, along with National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) teams, were swiftly deployed to the site for rescue and relief efforts.
• The pilgrims were reportedly returning from a religious site when the accident took place, with preliminary investigations pointing towards possible driver fatigue or mechanical failure.
• The state government has announced ex-gratia payments for the victims' families and assured comprehensive medical treatment for the injured.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court to Review Implementation Challenges of Digital Personal Data Protection Act
GS2.explainer:

• Petitioners highlight concerns regarding the readiness of data fiduciaries, the establishment of the Data Protection Board, and the clarity of certain provisions.
• The court's intervention is crucial for ensuring effective enforcement of the act and safeguarding citizens' privacy rights in the digital age.
• Legal experts anticipate the ruling will provide much-needed guidance on the operational aspects and potential ambiguities within the landmark legislation.
• The case underscores the judiciary's role in overseeing the execution of significant legislative reforms impacting fundamental rights.

• Agreements were reached to bolster joint exercises, technology transfer, and collaborative research and development in defence.
• The dialogue underscored the shared commitment to multilateralism and addressing global challenges, including climate change and counter-terrorism.

• GKVY targets school dropouts and unemployed youth in rural areas, offering certified courses in various trades relevant to the rural economy and emerging sectors.
• The scheme emphasizes public-private partnerships and leveraging digital platforms for wider outreach and efficient delivery of training programs.
• This initiative is expected to address the skill gap in rural India, reduce rural-urban migration, and boost rural entrepreneurship.

• The policy focuses on strengthening research and development, ensuring quality control of AYUSH products, and expanding educational and clinical facilities.
• It seeks to leverage the potential of AYUSH systems for preventive healthcare, wellness, and managing chronic diseases, complementing modern medicine.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Ocean Monitoring Satellite, Enhancing Maritime Security and Climate Research
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched a new advanced ocean monitoring satellite from Sriharikota, marking another milestone in India's space program.
• The satellite is equipped with high-resolution sensors to provide critical data on sea surface temperature, ocean currents, and marine ecosystems, vital for climate research and disaster prediction.
• This mission will significantly enhance India's capabilities in maritime domain awareness, assisting coastal security and naval operations.
• The data collected will also be crucial for fisheries management, monsoon prediction, and understanding the impacts of climate change on oceans.
• The launch further solidifies India's position as a leader in space technology for societal applications and environmental monitoring.

Examine the role of indigenous space technology in achieving self-reliance and global leadership.

GS3.imageDescription: A rocket lifting off from a launchpad, with a stylized representation of ocean waves and a satellite orbiting Earth.
GS3.trivia: The first Indian satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

GS3.headline: National Mission for Clean Ganga Releases Progress Report, Highlights Achievements in River Rejuvenation
GS3.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) has released its annual progress report, detailing significant strides in the rejuvenation of the Ganga River and its tributaries.
• The report highlights achievements in wastewater treatment infrastructure, solid waste management along the river banks, and afforestation drives.
• Increased community participation and public awareness campaigns have been crucial in fostering a sense of ownership for the 'Namami Gange' program.
• Challenges remain in addressing industrial pollution and ensuring sustained maintenance of assets, necessitating continued vigilance and integrated efforts.
• The mission aims to extend its focus to tributary rejuvenation and wetlands conservation, adopting a holistic approach to river basin management.

Analyze the socio-economic and ecological impact of a cleaner Ganga on the riparian communities.
GS3.imageDescription: A clean stretch of the Ganga River with newly built ghats and green banks, symbolizing rejuvenation efforts.
GS3.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populated river basin in the world, supporting over 500 million people.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy Framework to Boost MSME Exports
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) has launched a new policy framework aimed at significantly boosting exports from the sector.
• The policy includes provisions for easier access to credit, export promotion schemes, technological upgrades, and participation in international trade fairs.
• Special focus will be given to integrating MSMEs into global value chains and providing training for export readiness and compliance with international standards.
• This initiative is expected to enhance India's overall export competitiveness and contribute to the 'Make in India' vision by empowering smaller enterprises.
• Industry bodies have welcomed the move, emphasizing its potential to create jobs and foster economic diversification.

GS3.trivia: MSMEs contribute approximately 30% to India's GDP and around 48% to the country's exports.

GS3.headline: RBI Monetary Policy Committee Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest review meeting, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision comes amidst persistent concerns over retail inflation, despite signs of moderation in certain sectors of the economy.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, indicating a cautious 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Analysts suggest that the RBI is closely monitoring global economic developments and domestic demand conditions before considering any policy rate changes.
• The stability in the repo rate aims to provide certainty to financial markets and support the economic recovery trajectory.

Discuss the interplay between fiscal policy (Union Budget) and monetary policy (RBI) in managing the Indian economy.

GS3.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Environment Introduces Policy to Promote Circular Economy Principles
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has introduced a new national policy aimed at promoting circular economy principles across various sectors.
• The policy encourages waste reduction, reuse, recycling, and resource efficiency, moving away from the traditional linear 'take-make-dispose' model.
• Key provisions include incentives for industries adopting circular practices, development of eco-design standards, and promotion of green procurement.
• This initiative is expected to reduce environmental degradation, conserve natural resources, and create new economic opportunities in green industries.
• Stakeholders emphasize the need for robust regulatory frameworks and technological innovation to successfully transition towards a circular economy.

GS3.headline: New Report Highlights Accelerated Melting of Himalayan Glaciers, Urges Urgent Action
GS3.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by a consortium of environmental scientists highlights an alarming acceleration in the melting rate of Himalayan glaciers.
• The study attributes the intensified melting to rising global temperatures and localized black carbon deposition, threatening water security for millions.
• The rapid glacial retreat poses severe risks of increased flash floods, subsequent water scarcity, and long-term ecological imbalance in the region.
• Scientists call for urgent and concerted efforts to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and implement targeted conservation strategies in the Himalayan ecosystem.
• The report emphasizes the critical need for regional cooperation among Himalayan nations to address this transboundary environmental challenge.

Disaster and disaster management.

Evaluate the effectiveness of current national and international efforts in mitigating climate change impacts on fragile mountain ecosystems.
Examine the challenges in ensuring water security for downstream populations amidst changing glacial dynamics.
Suggest a comprehensive strategy involving policy, technology, and regional cooperation to address the crisis of melting Himalayan glaciers.
GS3.imageDescription: A photograph of a visibly receding Himalayan glacier with bare rock exposed, symbolizing glacial melt.
GS3.trivia: The Himalayas contain the third-largest deposit of ice and snow in the world, after the Arctic and Antarctic, earning them the nickname 'The Third Pole'.

GS3.headline: India's Digital Payments Ecosystem Witnessing Unprecedented Growth: Report
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by a leading financial consultancy indicates unprecedented growth in India's digital payments ecosystem, driven by UPI and government initiatives.
• The volume and value of digital transactions have surged across urban and rural areas, reflecting increasing adoption by consumers and merchants.
• Factors contributing to this growth include widespread smartphone penetration, accessible internet, and supportive regulatory frameworks.
• The report highlights the role of fintech innovations and the 'Digital India' program in fostering financial inclusion and a less-cash economy.
• Challenges like digital literacy, cybersecurity risks, and infrastructure gaps in remote areas still need to be addressed for sustained growth.

GS3.headline: India's First Private Sector Developed Cryogenic Rocket Engine Successfully Tested
GS3.explainer:
• In a significant stride for India's private space sector, a privately developed cryogenic rocket engine has successfully undergone its first test firing.
• This achievement marks a major step towards private players contributing to India's space launch capabilities, reducing reliance on ISRO for all components.
• The engine, developed by a Bengaluru-based startup, demonstrates the growing maturity and innovation within India's space tech ecosystem.
• This success is expected to attract further investment into the private space sector, fostering competition and accelerating technological advancements.
• It aligns with the government's push for greater private participation in space, aiming to make India a global hub for commercial space launches.

GS3.headline: Government Launches National Urban Air Mobility Policy to Boost Drone and Air Taxi Ecosystem
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Civil Aviation has unveiled India's first National Urban Air Mobility (NUAM) Policy, aiming to regulate and promote the use of drones and air taxis for urban transportation.
• The policy outlines a framework for safe operation, infrastructure development, air traffic management, and skill development for the emerging urban air mobility sector.
• This initiative is expected to revolutionize intra-city logistics, emergency services, and passenger transport, reducing congestion and travel times.
• It seeks to position India as a leader in drone technology and air taxi services, attracting investment and fostering innovation in the aerospace industry.
• Challenges include ensuring public safety, managing airspace, and developing robust charging infrastructure for electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft.
